extra.reasoning:
The property requires that flipping stiff_neck from false to true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity, given enum order [self_care < routine < urgent < emergent].

stiff_neck appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100), which requires hemoptysis AND fever AND sweating AND abdominal_pain AND leg_swelling AND chest_pain AND palpitations AND severe_headache AND back_pain AND stiff_neck, and sets acuity := self_care (the lowest level). It appears in no individual raise rule.

Take the baseline where the other 10 conjuncts of interaction_drop are all true and stiff_neck = false (syncope irrelevant, set false):
- BEFORE (stiff_neck=false): interaction_drop cannot fire (missing stiff_neck). The matching rules include priority-3 rules raise_fever, raise_abdominal_pain, raise_severe_headache, raise_chest_pain (all set emergent) plus several priority-2 urgent rules. Highest priority (3) wins -> acuity = emergent.
- AFTER (stiff_neck=true): all ten conjuncts of interaction_drop now hold, so it fires at priority 100, which dominates every other rule (priorities 2-3) -> acuity := self_care.

Acuity moves from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest) purely by setting stiff_neck true. That is a strict decrease, so monotonicity is violated. The witness_assignment gives the after-state (stiff_neck=true) input; comparing it to the same input with stiff_neck=false demonstrates the downward jump from emergent to self_care.
